lesson.menu_desc: Future: will / going to / present continuous -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- Plans & Predictions -- Future (will / going to / present continuous)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 4 -- Plans & Predictions

listenings.text: So, here are my plans for the year. I'm starting a new project at work next month. It's a big decision, but I feel ready. I'm probably going to travel to Portugal in October, because my brother lives there now. Hopefully, the flights won't be too expensive. I also have a personal goal: I want to run a half marathon. I expect it to be hard, but I'm training every morning. And next weekend? Next weekend I'm just going to relax. No plans at all. Sometimes that's the best plan.

This year I'm focusing on one big goal: I'm going to learn to cook properly. I've already arranged cooking classes near my home, and they start soon. I'm a little nervous, but I'm sure it will be fun. I'm also planning to spend more time with my friends. We're meeting next Saturday to plan a trip together. I probably won't travel far this year, but I expect to visit the coast at least once. Hopefully, by December, I'll feel proud of everything I tried. That's my plan, and I'm excited about it.

extra_audio.text: First, Sandra thinks about her goals for the year. Then she makes a decision about a new course. After that, she arranges the classes online. Next, she tells her friend about her plans. Finally, she feels excited about the future.
